short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: Functional programming is introduced using Haskell.  The standard programming techniques, as well as some advanced topics, are covered and applied to realistic programming problems.

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: 3 hours per week: 2x 1 hour lectures, 1x 1 hour lab

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value:
Functional programming languages are one of the central focuses of modern research in programming languages, and they are also of immense practical utility.  This course presents the concepts of functional programming using Haskell, which is one of the most widely used functional languages.  The emphasis is on developing practical programming skills, applying advanced techniques such as monads, parallelism, metaprogramming, and formal methods.
The aims are to understand the principles of functional programming, including functions, expressions, types, type inference, equational reasoning, and monads, and to develop the ability to solve substantial programming problems using Haskell.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value:
Examination 60%, Set Exercises 40%. 

Set exercises will include a programming project and a class test.

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value: Set exercises, with interactive feedback online. Discussion forums and multiple choice quizzes. Peer review of source code.

additional_relevant_information.label: Additional Relevant Information
additional_relevant_information.value: The resit exam can only be taken by Masters students.
